commit.message:
Initial scaffold: Hono + D1 + Drizzle with full API parity

Cloudflare Workers alternative to parable-service (Clojure/DigitalOcean).

- Hono.js framework with TypeScript
- Cloudflare D1 (SQLite) with Drizzle ORM schema for all 15 tables
- Full route parity: health, auth, stories CRUD, puritan library (FTS5),
  assessments, weather proxy, MCP protocol
- Kinde OAuth + JWT + API key authentication middleware
- Server-rendered HTML views with TailwindCSS + Alpine.js + HTMX
- Vitest test setup with @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers
- GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with Wrangler deploy
- Zod schema validation for all request/response types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Sprint 12 QA — Wizard API, confirm(), EntityTable actions, CLI await

Critical bug fixes found during QA review of Sprint 12:

Wizard API mismatch (pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Rewrote all wizard steps from { title, fields } to { label, render(data) }
- Wizard expects render functions returning HTML with data-field attrs
- Added initialData, validate functions, proper select rendering

confirm() signature (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Changed confirm('string') to confirm({ title, message, ... })
- confirm() destructures an object param, not a string

EntityTable actions (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Actions onClick receives single item from row menu, not array
- Wrapped single items in arrays: onClick: (item) => handler([item])

CLIService (alertCmd):
- Added async/await to state.update call in alert resolve

StateEngine seed data:
- Fixed proj-001 vpcs reference from 'Dev-VPC' to 'vpc-002' (UUID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track OpenSSF Scorecards for all repos (#126)

Signed-off-by: Jessica G <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: production hardening, security audit, tests, refactor

- Security: SecurityHeadersMiddleware, JWT hardening, rate limiting, admin Basic Auth
- Secrets: removed hardcoded keys, docker-compose uses required env vars, .env.example
- Docker: non-root users in API and AdminWeb Dockerfiles
- Program.cs refactor: 975 -> 212 lines, extracted 9 endpoint groups to Endpoints/
- BotTickHostedService: fixed duplicate, now uses correct Application layer impl

commit.message:
Add KQL engine, query telemetry, DML commands, vector search, and Explorer enhancements

New features:
- KQL query engine with operators (where, project, extend, summarize, sort, top, take, distinct, count)
- Query telemetry store and activity tracking interfaces and storage implementations
- DML command service for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Vector embedding policy and vector index models
- KQL query controller and query telemetry controller in Host

Explorer (React):
- Container settings panel
- Import documents dialog
- KQL query editor component
- Query telemetry dashboard

Query engine improvements:
- Additional system functions and scalar subquery support
- Enhanced query explain service

Tests:
- KQL query executor tests
- DML command service, consistency middleware, indexing directive tests
- System function, scalar subquery, trigger execution, UDF query tests
- Vector search, unique key, query telemetry, TTL cleanup tests

feat: configurable storage backend with Explorer settings UI

Add support for selecting between SurrealDb, SQLite, and InMemory storage
backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

---------

commit.message:
Add naming conventions and fix all naming inconsistencies

New: docs/naming-conventions.md
- Canonical lexicon: one word per concept (entities, actions, properties, references)
- Banned synonyms table: never use 'remove' (use 'delete'), 'link' (use 'edge'), etc.
- Flag suffix rules: -id for UUIDs, -name for names, -type for type filters
- No-abbreviation rule with explicit allowed exceptions (id, ctx, db, tx, err)
- Cross-layer consistency matrix (CLI flag → JSON → DB column → Go field)
- Go naming patterns: type names, method names, field names, acronym casing
- Direction values: outgoing/incoming/both (not out/in)

Fixed across all docs:
- CLI: --from/--to/--node → --from-id/--to-id/--node-id (UUID flags must end with -id)
- CLI: --tag → --tag-name (for tag name) / --tag-id (for tag UUID)
- CLI: --depth → --max-depth (unambiguous)
- CLI: direction 'out'/'in' → 'outgoing'/'incoming' (full words)
- CLI: gm node tag/untag now uses --tag-id flag (not stdin JSON)
- JSON: from_ref/to_ref → from_reference/to_reference (no abbreviations)
- Go: QueryInput → GraphQueryInput, TraverseInput → GraphTraverseInput
- Go: QueryResult → GraphQueryResult, TraverseResult → GraphTraverseResult
- Go: CommitResult → ProposalCommitResult
- Go: TraverseResult.Root → RootID (consistent -ID suffix)
- Architecture: added tag package to dependency graph and system diagram
- Conventions: added tag.go to model/ and tag.go to cli/ in project structure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct SAML login sessions column names

COLS.samlLoginSessions had wrong columns (relay_state, request_id, expires_at)
that don't exist in the saml_login_sessions table. Fixed to match actual schema:
id, tenant_id, provider_id, attorney_id, name_id, name_id_format, session_index,
created_at, updated_at.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add architecture design — layers, data flow, interfaces, lifecycle

- Vision recap: 5 core thesis statements
- System layers: CLI → Service → Model → DB → SQLite
- Package architecture with strict dependency rules (no cycles)
- Data flow: read path (direct projection), write path (event + projection
  in one TX), proposal flow (create → validate → commit atomic)
- Event sourcing mechanics: event types, inline projection, rebuild path
- Graph operations: traversal (recursive CTE), validation (cycles, FK, types)
- Key Go interfaces: GraphService, ProposalService, EventService
- Concrete implementations with dependency injection via constructors
- Initialization lifecycle: open DB → migrate → wire services → execute
- Error propagation: SQLite → service (domain errors) → CLI (JSON + exit code)
- SQLite-specific: single conn pool, WAL, embedded migrations, JSON ops
- Document map linking all docs together

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: graphmind
repository.description: A local-first, AI-native project management CLI that models work as an evolving graph—turning raw context into structured tasks, relationships, and insights through conversational modeling.

commit.message:
docs: add Go & database conventions

- Go 1.26 target, use new language features (new(expr), self-ref generics)
- Project structure: cmd/, internal/ (db, model, graph, proposal, event, cli)
- Core deps: modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go), google/uuid (UUID v7), cobra (CLI)
- No ORM, no CGO — database/sql + raw SQL
- SQLite PRAGMAs: WAL, foreign keys, busy timeout, cache size
- Data type mapping: UUID→TEXT, timestamps→ISO8601 TEXT, JSON→TEXT
- Naming: plural tables, snake_case columns, idx_ prefixed indexes
- Schema migrations: embedded SQL, forward-only, auto-applied at startup
- Full 001_init.sql with events, nodes, edges, proposals, schema_version
- Error handling: standard errors, sentinel vars, fmt.Errorf wrapping
- Testing: stdlib only, in-memory SQLite, table-driven
- Code style: gofmt, go vet, context-first, snake_case JSON tags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: scorpio_ws
repository.description: Full workspace for scorpio-robot, containing simulations, drivers, applications

commit.message:
docs: add CLI specification — AI-friendly-first design

- Define what 'AI-friendly' means concretely (vs traditional CLI)
- Core contract: stdout is always JSON, exit codes are semantic
- Output envelope: {ok, data, error} with typed error codes
- Command structure: gm <resource> <action> [flags] [< stdin]
- Full command reference: node, edge, proposal, event, graph, schema
- gm schema: self-describing CLI — AI agents discover API at runtime
- Input conventions: flags for lookups, stdin JSON for mutations
- Built-in node types (task, epic, decision, risk, release, discussion)
- Built-in edge types (depends_on, blocks, decompose, caused_by, etc.)
- Proposal create with cross-reference support (from_ref/to_ref)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add technical design — SQLite + adjacency list + event sourcing

- Storage choice: SQLite (rationale + rejected alternatives)
- Data model: events (source of truth), nodes/edges (projections), proposals
- UUID v7 primary keys, JSON properties, recursive CTE traversal
- Cycle detection strategy
- CLI JSON output format for AI agents

commit.message:
docs: rewrite README — unified structure, clear hierarchy

Restructured from scratch with a clear logical flow:
  1. What — AI-agent-native graph project management tool
  2. Why — traditional tools lose information by oversimplifying
  3. Architecture — Human ↔ AI Agent ↔ CLI ↔ Graph layers
  4. Workflow — concrete 6-step example with CLI commands
  5. Design Principles — consolidated into a single table
  6. AI Role — complexity rectifier, not decision maker
  7. Non-goals / Tech stack

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
